summary: FOMC voted to hold benchmark funds rate at 3.5%-3.75% on April 29, 2026. Unusual 8-4 split: Miran wanted a 25bp cut; Hammack, Kashkari, Logan opposed easing bias. Inflation elevated partly from elevated energy prices; job gains low. Middle East conflict cited as major uncertainty. Powell signaled he will remain on Board of Governors indefinitely despite political pressure.
